# Office Display Manager — Business logic

## Business rules

<!-- BEGIN:generated:business-rules -->
| Rule | Table | When | Actions | Order | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ODM: unique active slideshow per account | `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | before | insert, update | 100 | Rejects a second active slideshow for the same service account. The player resolves exactly one deck per screen. |
| ODM: maintain player link | `x_804244_odm_slideshow` | before | insert, update | 200 | Sets the read-only player_link field from the assigned service account |
<!-- END:generated:business-rules -->

## Script includes

<!-- BEGIN:generated:script-includes -->
| Name | Accessible from | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `OdmPlayerPage` | package_private | Renders the ODM player HTML for a screen (used by the x_804244_odm_player.do direct UI page) |

## Application code

<!-- BEGIN:generated:app-code -->
#### `src/server/deck.ts`

| Export | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `interface Slide` | ODM deck building — pure functions only (no Glide APIs) so jest can cover every branch. |
| `interface Deck` |  |
| `parseLinks(raw)` | Parse the `links` field into slide URLs. |
| `toPositiveInt(value, fallback, min)` | Coerce a numeric field with a floor and default. |
| `interface SlideshowFields` | Raw record fields as strings (what GlideRecord.getValue returns). |
| `buildDeck(screen, fields)` | Build the deck object served to the player. |
| `escapeDeckJson(deck)` | Serialize a deck for injection into the HTML template. |
| `injectDeck(templateHtml, deck)` | Inject the deck into the template HTML. |

#### `src/server/handlers.src.ts`

| Export | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `renderPlayerHtml(screen)` | Server-side render for the direct UI page (/x_804244_odm_player.do): the browser gets the finished document, nothing client-side. |
| `serveDeckJson(request, response)` | GET /player/deck?screen=... |
<!-- END:generated:app-code -->
